title: iris aphylla subsp. dacica
intro: Iris aphylla subsp. dacica, also known as the Dacian iris, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the iris family, native to Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Iris aphylla subsp. dacica include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its potential to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions. Iris aphylla subsp. dacica can be prepared as an infusion or decoction for its medicinal uses, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
